mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a strengthening labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. This is an always-on trend signal.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of periods when PAYEMS month-over-month change is positive, or Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.0%, it signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer income and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or if the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates demand-supply imbalance. When RSAFS growth outpaces INDPRO growth, it signals potential overheating and future tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the ratio RSAFS/INDPRO and 20-day forward returns is not statistically significant (|Rank IC| < 0.04 over any 3-year window).

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts (HOUST) act as a leading indicator of economic slowdown, as housing investment is sensitive to interest rates and confidence. A sustained drop signals lower future demand and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle housing downturn (HOUST < 1800 for 3 consecutive months)
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods when HOUST is in a 3-month decline (month-over-month negative for 3 months), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A high level of consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 198 signals persistent inflationary pressure, which prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, reducing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If mean 5-day forward return after CPIAUCSL > 198 is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or is positive, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing activity and economic growth, which supports corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. This is an always-on trend signal.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds 0.2%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously low unemployment (UNRATE < 4.5) and high payroll employment (PAYEMS > 134,000 thousand) indicate a very tight labor market, which may lead to wage inflation pressuring corporate margins and eventually negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the signal (UNRATE < 4.5 and PAYEMS > 134,000) does not produce a mean 60-day forward return significantly less than zero (p < 0.05 over 5-year rolling windows), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in retail sales (RSAFS) below 350,000 million dollars signals a sharp contraction in consumer spending, which is a primary driver of economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of occurrences when RSAFS falls below 350,000, or if the average return is not statistically different from zero (p > 0.10).
